title: alchemilla vulgaris var. diversiloba
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. diversiloba, also known as lady's mantle, is a type of flowering plant in the rosaceae family, characterized by its distinctive rounded leaves and delicate white flowers. Traditionally, lady's mantle has been used in herbal medicine for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and promote wound healing. The medicinal properties of lady's mantle have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve overall health. Lady's mantle can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, menstrual disorders, and skin conditions.
